text.title: Contract Test, Integration Test, and Spec Validation Examples
text.summary: Concrete code examples for Pact consumer-side contract test, FastAPI integration test with negative cases, and OpenAPI response validation.

example.note: Consumer defines the interaction: what request it will send and what response shape it expects. Pact generates a pact file for the provider to verify.

section.title: FastAPI Integration Test with Negative Cases

example.note: Tests cover 201 happy path, 404 not-found, and 400 validation error — all in one class, sharing auth fixture.
Validates that the actual response body matches the schema declared in openapi.yaml — catches drift that status-code assertions miss.
example.code:
from openapi_core import OpenAPI from openapi_core.testing.mock import MockRequest, MockResponse
spec = OpenAPI.from_file_path("openapi.yaml")
def test_list_users_matches_spec(client, auth_headers): response = client.get("/users", headers=auth_headers) mock_req = MockRequest("http://localhost", "GET", "/users") mock_resp = MockResponse(data=response.content, status_code=response.status_code) result = spec.validate_response(mock_req, mock_resp) assert not result.errors, f"Schema violations: {result.errors}"

antipattern.description: Asserting only status code: assert response.status_code == 200
antipattern.reason: Does not catch body schema drift, missing required fields, or wrong types. Add validate_response(spec, response) and at least one body field assertion.

antipattern.description: Hardcoded primary keys like user_id="1" shared across test functions
antipattern.reason: Collides across parallel test runs and masks order-dependent bugs. Use factories that generate unique values.
